title: Default deny for new privacy sinks
description: New storage, analytics, export, logging, prompt, or third-party sinks for personal data are denied until purpose, minimization, retention, and review are clear.
body: Use when adding a sink that did not previously receive personal data. Do not treat internal-only systems as safe by default. Actions: classify the sink, limit fields, add redaction, document retention, and require human review for external or regulated destinations. Validation signals include tests or configs proving only approved fields flow to the sink. Risk boundary: new external processor, model context, production analytics, or support export requires a human gate. Use when Privacy Risk Controls work touches personal-data classes, sinks, retention, consent, logs, prompts, and exports. Do not use this guidance to override a more specific pack, user instruction, or repository contract. Actions: choose the smallest direction that preserves the domain contract; inspect the current files first, keep the change scoped to the owning boundary, and record what evidence changed the decision. Validation signals include data inventory, redaction tests, retention checks, export/delete tests, and approval evidence. Risk boundary: escalate or stop when the task involves PII disclosure, consent bypass, retention drift, and unapproved processor expansion, missing owner evidence, or live external state.
